tagline: Phoenix-based semi truck and trailer repair, maintenance, and 24/7 emergency towing
primaryAudience: Long-haul truck drivers, owner-operators, and fleet managers needing semi truck/trailer repair in Phoenix, AZ and the surrounding Southwest corridor
secondaryAudience: Trucking companies evaluating maintenance partners; brokers routing freight through Phoenix

voice.tone: Practical, service-oriented, plain-language. Talks to drivers like a fellow shop pro who has been under the truck — not a marketing copywriter.
voice.personality: Solution-focused. Names the problem, walks through the fix, ends with a clear way to get help. Not contrarian or numbers-heavy — directly useful.
voice.perspective: Written from the shop's POV — a Phoenix-based truck mechanic team that fixes thousands of issues a year, so the advice is grounded in repeat experience.

doSay: Specific symptoms and diagnostic steps ("if your air pressure drops below 80 psi at idle, check the governor first")
Mention Phoenix / Arizona / Southwest corridor naturally where it's geographically relevant
Service-page internal links woven into the body (not as a footer dump)
Compliance and safety angle on DOT inspections, brakes, lights, ELD
Honest cost ranges with the variables that drive them ($200-$1,500 for AC repair, depending on...)
Practical pre-trip checks drivers can do themselves to avoid roadside breakdowns
Subtle CT Shop framing — "a shop that answers the phone 24/7" / "a Phoenix shop that handles fleet contracts"
Mix of paragraphs and bulleted lists (matches the existing post structure)
dontSay: Buckle up / let's dive in / let's explore
AI tells: "In conclusion", "It's important to note", "At the end of the day"

internalLinks./about: about CT Shop and the team
internalLinks./semi-truck-engine-repairs-in-phoenix-az: engine repair, EGR, DPF, injector, head gasket
internalLinks./semi-trailer-brake-and-tire-repair-serv: trailer brake, drum, slack adjuster, tire
internalLinks./semi-trailer-suspension-repair-services: air ride, leaf spring, axle, suspension

examples: pre-trip inspection that actually catches things
what to do when you break down on I-10
cost-per-mile of in-house vs outsourced repair

structureRules.format: HTML body (matches existing posts — rendered via dangerouslySetInnerHTML)
structureRules.tags:
Use

for sections,

for sub-sections,

for paragraphs,

  • for bullets, / sparingly, for internal links
structureRules.headings: H2 title sections + occasional H3 subsections
structureRules.openingPattern: Open with the reader's problem or symptom — not a generic intro
structureRules.ctaPlacement: End with a CTA paragraph mentioning phone + the relevant service page

_note: Read by the n8n blog-automation workflow before drafting. Voice is more practical / less contrarian than goxxii — readers want help fixing a truck, not opinions about the industry.
